concept: Expression / The Outward Flow of Being
description: The act of bringing an inner state—a thought, feeling, or idea—into an outer, perceivable form. It is the bridge between the inner world and the outer world.
rationale: The word begins with 'sho', the gentle fricative that carries inner truth outward into form. This flows through 'mi', acknowledging the personal source from which all authentic expression springs. The final 'ra' radiates this manifested truth into the world, giving it lasting presence and reach. This three-part journey—from breath carrying truth, through personal essence, to radiant manifestation—captures expression as both process and artifact. The sound progression feels like thought becoming form: the soft exhalation of 'sho', the inner resonance of 'mi', and the outward rippling of 'ra'. Each syllable builds on the last, creating a word that embodies the transformation of inner world into outer reality.
